selected_important_turn.text: Okay, it sounds like we are pretty agreeable here.  I will wait for you to make an offer if you would like to.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn ceded initiative at a critical moment. THE M counterpart had just restated preferences and invited YOU to propose; instead YOU deferred, and the opponent immediately submitted a deal. By not proposing a concrete, integrative offer, YOU missed the chance to shape terms that better reflect both parties' priorities.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is cooperative in tone but passive in impact. It leaves the deal content to the other side (who favors food/firewood), risking an outcome that favors THEM and foregoing creative trade packages that could increase joint value and fairness. It's a high-leverage missed opportunity because the next turn closed the negotiation.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Take initiative: convert cooperative tone into a concrete proposal or a small menu of package options that trade water (your high priority) for food/firewood (their high priorities), or at least ask a targeted tradeoff question (e.g., which of food or firewood would you be willing to reduce to give me more water?).

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase (by enabling integrative trades instead of default split)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: likely increase (prevents opponent-unilateral framing; allows distribution that better reflects disclosed needs)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: likely increase (clear options reduce rounds and framing uncertainty)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: no_change_to_increase (offers collaborative leadership while still cooperative)

value2reason.Medium: To be able to cook the food.

value2reason.Low: I brought enough food to last and I'm also on a diet!
value2reason.Medium: I absolutely love campfires, and I always use tons of wood, so I need as much wood as I can get!
value2reason.High: A person can survive without food for a certain period of time, but you can't survive without water for very long.

dialogue.text: What are your preferences?  I always love having a big fire at night when I am camping and I do think water is more important than food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I like to have a lot of food to keep my energy up, but I might need some firewood to cook that food. I can't wait for my camping trip.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I love camping also!  Yes, I can see how you would need firewood if you want to cook your food.  So, food is more important to you than water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yes, I will need a little water to wash that food down with though. Are you going to be needing a lot of water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes, I may need more water.  I am on a diet and I don't really cook either!  Hopefully we can work something out here.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That sounds great. I probably need to go on a diet, but I like my food to much. LOL
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Well, I am pre-diabetic, so I had to do it for my health. I love food, too...especially sugar!  🙂🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Oh, that does change things a lot. Diabetes is a hard disease to have to deal with. So you want more firewood than water? And less food than water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Well, I don't know.  I think I would like more water than firewood, but yes less food than water.  I'm a pretty easy going person though! 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Ok, let me know what you think and we will go from there. I do prefer more food and firewood than water though.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Okay, it sounds like we are pretty agreeable here.  I will wait for you to make an offer if you would like to.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'd like to propose a trade. Since I value water most, would you be willing to give up one food package for an extra water package? That way, we both get what we need without losing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade one food for one water, but I'd prefer to keep more food overall. Let's split the food evenly and then adjust with firewood or water as needed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your willingness to trade, but I need more water than food. How about this: I take 2 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood, and you take 1 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood? It balances our priorities better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think splitting food evenly makes sense for me, but I need to ensure I get enough water. Let’s try 2 food, 1 water, and 1 firewood for me, and 1 food, 2 water, and 2 firewood for you. That way, we both get what we need without sacrificing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy with your proposal. Let's confirm: I take 1 food, 2 water, and 2 firewood, and you take 2 food, 1 water, and 1 firewood. That seems fair and meets both our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed: I take 2 food, 1 water, and 1 firewood. You take 1 food, 2 water, and 2 firewood. Safe camping!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
